doc: A self-playing saw arpeggio through the DJ-mixer `djfilter` knob. The Clock's beat gate drives a sequencer (a major arpeggio) into a 4-voice Voicer; each voice fires a saw oscillator through `/djfilter` and an ADSR into the output. The star is the one bipolar filter knob: a public identity `map` (`/filter_knob`, rests at North = 0) smoothed through an `m2s` into the djfilter's Signal `position` input — so turning it is zipper-free. Send /filter_knob [-1..1]: 0 = open/no filter, negative (CCW) sweeps a low-pass down, positive (CW) sweeps a high-pass up. Set tempo via /clock/tempo; edit the line via /sequencer/step1../sequencer/step8.

nodes.doc: Walks a major arpeggio one step per beat -> degree Messages.
Saw tone (rich harmonics for the filter to chew).
The DJ filter. position is driven by the smoothed knob; resonance set to sing on sweeps.
Plucky ADSR envelope (linear CV), gated per step.
Shape the linear envelope CV into an exponential-style volume curve (x^2).
PUBLIC DJ filter knob. Identity pass-through over [-1,1]; rests at North = 0 (emitted on init so the chain and any control surface agree at rest). Send /filter_knob [-1..1].
Smooth the knob into a CV signal feeding the djfilter position; default 0 (North) before any knob move.
